tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows evidence of renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia (low glucose and HbA1c), and significant proteinuria with ketonuria. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) suggest possible urinary tract pathology, but no infection markers (nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend urgent evaluation for acute or chronic kidney disease, assessment of hypoglycemia causes, and further investigation of urinary abnormalities.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
